topic: Memory leaks and process lifecycle hazards in .opencode/skills/mcp-coco-index and .opencode/skills/system-code-graph during CLI deep-flow orchestration

focus: Inventory mcp-coco-index process spawn, daemon, sidecar, local model, indexing, and cleanup surfaces
Retained in-process state behind iter-001: Project.close, registry caches, code-graph shutdown, embedder HTTP clients
noveltyJustification: Five new findings (F-011..F-015) plus two confirmations and one refinement of iter-001; cleanup-asymmetry between __init__ and close_all is new evidence not in iter-001.
summary: Project.close releases only the sqlite-vec ManagedConnection; ProjectRegistry leaks embedders + load-time events on close_all; system-code-graph shutdown never calls closeDb; LiteLLM embedder relies on upstream module-globals.

focus: Confirm cleanup-asymmetry diff is exhaustive; audit _bg_index fire-and-forget pattern in cli.py and server.py
noveltyJustification: Two new MCP-child-side findings (F-017, F-018) plus a tightened refinement of F-014 (F-016); cluster reframing of F-005/F-010 to span daemon AND MCP child processes is new.
summary: Cleanup-asymmetry diff confirmed exhaustive at 7 caches; only _embedder_by_config_hash is a true deterministic leak under refresh. Fire-and-forget _bg_index repeated at cli.py:1204 and server.py:557 with bare except + ThreadPoolExecutor hazard.

focus: DaemonClient.index cancellation semantics + protocol cancel-surface audit + remaining create_task survey
noveltyJustification: Three new findings (F-019 blocking RPC with no abort, F-020 missing CancelIndexRequest, F-021 foreground worker-survives-cancel) plus cluster-split reframing F-017/F-018 as caller-side symptom of F-019/F-020 protocol gap.
summary: DaemonClient.index is fully blocking with no cancellation surface; protocol exposes only StopRequest and RemoveProjectRequest; foreground refresh handlers carry same ThreadPool-survives-cancel hazard. F-017/F-018 remediation split: protocol-level cancel packet is prerequisite for task-discipline packet.

noveltyJustification: Three new findings (F-022 RemoveProject unsafe under concurrent Index, F-023 _ADAPTERS no close path with measured per-session accumulation rate, F-024 sidecar fallback-pin hazard) plus closure of two iter-004 tail questions and a five-packet remediation synthesis ready for Codex-lane refinement.
summary: RemoveProjectRequest cannot be repurposed as a cancel path: it deletes registry pointers under an active indexer with no task cancel, opening KeyError + double-indexer + sqlite-vec-closed-under-write races. _ADAPTERS accumulates ≤4 heavy adapter entries per session with zero close path; HttpSidecarRerankerAdapter additionally pins an httpx.Client pool and (on first 5xx) a bundled CrossEncoder fallback inside the same daemon, neutering sidecar memory savings.

focus: Project.close depth and runtime VM/RSS feasibility
noveltyJustification: The iteration adds concrete upstream CocoIndex close-depth evidence plus live negative measurement bounds from the sandboxed runtime.
summary: ManagedConnection.close closes the target sqlite3 connection, but Project.close remains shallow for App/Environment/core state and unsafe under F-022 close-under-write; runtime RSS capture was blocked, so F-023/F-024 stay P2 pending unsandboxed measurement.

noveltyJustification: The pass adds unsandboxed process/RSS inventory and one new code-graph launcher lifecycle finding, but mostly refines severity and ordering for existing findings.
summary: Native measurement confirms detached daemon, sidecar, MCP child, code-graph, and lingering CLI processes with about 2.33 GiB combined RSS, but failed Homebrew ccc commands mean the log is process-inventory evidence rather than normal-search leak-growth evidence.

noveltyJustification: This iteration adds ticket-ready scopes, dependencies, verification gates, and merge/defer decisions without new source discovery.
summary: Drafted ten remediation packets ordered by correctness prerequisites, host lifecycle cleanup, and lower-priority resident-memory/read-path work.

focus: Final packet matrix and convergence synthesis
noveltyJustification: No new source discovery; novelty is the exact final matrix, F-004 fold decision, downgrade list, and convergence blocker check.
summary: Mapped F-001 through F-027 plus M-006/M-007 to final packets, severities, and verification gates while preserving correctness and lifecycle cleanup ahead of resident-memory optimization.

noveltyJustification: No new source discovery; novelty is the final report packaging, convergence statement, and separation of verification gaps from synthesis blockers.
summary: Finalized research.md with the iteration 009 matrix, packet ordering, downgrade notes, measurement limits, non-goals, and references.

reason: User requested five more cli-codex gpt-5.5 xhigh fast iterations on surfaced recommendations.

noveltyJustification: Source validation turns the prior packet ordering into concrete internal-registry, request-identity, timeout, and test-boundary requirements.
summary: Validated that remove-project safety should remain an internal prerequisite packet and protocol cancel should follow with explicit project/request identity.

focus: Validate bg-index lifecycle and code-graph owner/orphan recommendations
noveltyJustification: Source validation refines the umbrella background-index packet into process-boundary work and tightens orphan/ambiguous-liveness rules for code-graph ownership.
summary: Validated that daemon task ownership, MCP ThreadPool shutdown, and code-graph single-owner/orphan handling need separate acceptance gates despite sharing the lifecycle theme.

focus: Process sweep and rerank sidecar lifecycle safety
noveltyJustification: Source validation converts the process-sweep and sidecar recommendations into concrete inventory metadata, dry-run gates, ownership records, and unsafe pkill-pattern exclusions.
summary: Validated that process sweep and sidecar lifecycle can share inventory classifiers, but destructive actions must be exact-PID, owner-token gated, dry-run first, and service-policy-specific.

focus: Project, embedder cache, adapter lifecycle validation
noveltyJustification: Adds source-backed close API mapping and refines resident-memory packet ordering, adapter close shape, and benchmark gates without changing severity.
summary: Validated that registry embedder cleanup depends on remove safety, adapter cleanup needs explicit cache-owner eviction, and escalation above P2 still requires successful-search or fallback-growth measurement.

noveltyJustification: This pass adds the final refined order, split/merge decisions, readiness matrix, and exact first implementation packet from the already validated continuation evidence.
summary: Kept remove-project-cancel-safety first, split background-index work by process boundary, safety-gated sweep/sidecar actions, and marked adapter escalation as benchmark-gated.
